mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 65% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of months where unemployment jumps above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, driving sustained corporate earnings revisions and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDP and retail sales fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints and impending recession, causing negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during late-cycle phases yield positive 20-day returns in over 50% of cases, breaking the recessionary signal.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and generating positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth months produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.5% triggers immediate inflation fears and expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, forcing rapid equity deleveraging over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in 60% or more of months where CPI inflation surpasses the 0.5% monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales demonstrate synchronized strength in durable goods and consumer discretionary spending, forecasting positive quarterly market performance.
failure_condition: Combined housing and retail sales growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of quarters during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment alongside a flat or rising federal funds rate indicates stagflationary pressure, destroying corporate margins and triggering negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising unemployment and non-falling rates yields positive 20-day returns in over 50% of observed months, invalidating the stagflation penalty.
